question_text: Which statement best explains why a pencil appears bent when placed in a glass of water?

options.label: A
options.text: Light is absorbed by the water

options.label: B
options.text: Light rays bend when passing from water to air, making the pencil appear to be in a different position

options.label: C
options.text: The water is cold and makes the pencil contract

options.label: D
options.text: The pencil is actually bent but straightens again when removed

explanation: Refraction occurs at the water-air boundary. Light from the pencil bends as it leaves the water, causing our brain to perceive the pencil as being bent or displaced from its actual position.
